title: humboltia elongata
intro: Humboldtia elongata, also known as the Poison Tree or Devil's Tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in India and Southeast Asia. The plant contains a toxic compound called homobasilocin, which has medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. The health benefits of Humboldtia elongata include its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential in modern medicine to develop new antibiotics. Humboldtia elongata can be prepared as a herbal medicine through various methods, including decoction, infusion, and ointment, although caution should be exercised due to its toxic nature.
